nodes.text:
You have come through your 21 days.

Before your 1:1 coaching call, we will walk through a short pre-diagnosis together. Five to seven minutes is enough.

Each line you write here goes directly to your coach.

The coach reads it beforehand and shapes the flow of the call around you.
From the 12 answers you have left, we will revisit a few we would like to look at more closely with you on the call. Shall we begin?

options.text: Give me a moment to settle
How is this used before the call?

nodes.text:
Take your time. There is no set pace for any answer.

When you feel ready, gently press [Continue].

Your responses here, along with your four free-form notes, are securely delivered to your coach before the call.

The coach keeps them at hand while preparing the 30 minutes that belong to you.

[Axis A · Mission alignment]

You answered that you were aware of your mission 'almost daily' or 'every few days.' Returning inward that often over 21 days means your mission has already become part of your daily breath.

[Axis A · Mission alignment]

About once a week, you said. That's not too few — it's measurably more than the version of you before these 21 days. Remember where the starting line was.

[Axis A · Mission alignment]

You said you rarely thought of your mission. Thank you for your honesty. These 21 days aren't about awareness shifts — they're about the fact that you began at all. No need for self-blame.

Your one sentence for 'the moment your mission felt clearest':

  "{{q3}}"

Let's pause here for a moment. This may be the firmest ground of the entire 21 days. Your coach will likely begin the call from this very sentence.
How did that land?

'Half is different' is often the most honest answer. 'All correct' often signals self-censorship; 'all wrong' often signals self-denial. I'll note this half-difference so your coach can follow it on the call.

That different feeling is also accurate data. Usually the diagnosis isn't wrong — a few days of change have slipped in between the version of you who answered and the version of you now. I'll let your coach know you want to revisit this.

⬛ One line for your coach — [Axis A · Mission]

Among what you thought about regarding your mission over the 21 days, write the one thing you most want your coach to name on the call. One sentence, one word, or blank — all are okay. (Optional, ~500 chars)

[Axis B · Execution of first actions]

You executed 2-3 of your first 3 actions. That's remarkable — 90% of starters stop in week one. You're outside that statistic.

[Axis B · Execution of first actions]

One or two of your first three. That's not 'less' — it's the footprint of genuine attempt. Only those who try can feel the friction.

[Axis B · Execution of first actions]

You said you barely executed any. Thank you for your honesty. On the coaching call, we won't ask 'why didn't you' — we'll explore 'what felt heaviest.' Time, priorities, or inner resistance?

Your note on 'the hardest moment':

  "{{q6}}"

Let's stay with this sentence for a moment. The first task of the next 3 weeks may live inside it. Your coach will follow this difficulty's grain with you on the call.
Looking back at your 21 days of action, what feeling feels closest right now?

options.text: I did enough, I feel relieved

nodes.text: 'Unfinishedness' is the strongest fuel for the next 3 weeks. If you were fully satisfied, there'd be nowhere to go. I'll pass this grain of unfinishedness to your coach precisely.

Still feeling heavy — this is usually not a sign that 21 days were insufficient, but a sign that 'a single person's help is needed here.' This will likely be the first place your coach asks about on the call.

⬛ One line for your coach — [Axis B · Action]

If you described your 21 days of execution in one sentence — what would it be? Tell your coach which part of this action you'd like to address on the call. (Optional, ~500 chars)

[Axis C · Clarity of the next 3 weeks]

Your picture for the next 3 weeks is clear, and you can put it in one sentence. This is the greatest asset of these 21 days — scattered intentions converged into a single line.

[Axis C · Clarity of the next 3 weeks]

The picture is clear, but compressing it into one sentence is still hard. That's fine — image first, words second. We can shape that one sentence together on the coaching call.

[Axis C · Clarity of the next 3 weeks]

You can put it in one sentence, yet the image is still misty. Interesting — usually it's the opposite. You're someone whose words pull the picture forward. On the call we'll expand that sentence into a picture.

[Axis C · Clarity of the next 3 weeks]

The picture for your next 3 weeks is still hazy. That's an honest answer. These 21 days aren't a 'planning phase' but a 'gathering phase.' Fog takes time to lift — and the coaching call is a place to look at that fog together.

Your one sentence for the next 3 weeks:

  "{{q9}}"

Please stay with this sentence for a moment. Remember — the version of you on day 21 wrote this. The version on day 28 will be clearer, and the coaching call is the place that brings that clarity sooner.
For the next 3 weeks — does one thing come to mind right now?

options.text: Two or three — I need to sort them

nodes.text: Two or three options is a good sign. 'I need to sort them' really means 'I need to choose' — and the coaching call's best 30 minutes are usually spent narrowing those choices together.
'Not yet' is also an accurate answer. The next 3 weeks can be about 'keeping a space open for the one thing to arise,' not 'finding the one thing.' Your coach will guard that empty space with you.

⬛ One line for your coach — [Axis C · Next 3 weeks]

When you imagine the next 3 weeks, write the one thing you most want to unpack with your coach on the call. (Optional, ~500 chars)

[Axis D · Assetization ⭐]

You said 'multiple changes have settled in' or 'the direction of life has shifted.' This is the deepest outcome a 21-day report can produce. Once changes settle, they don't scatter again.

[Axis D · Assetization ⭐]

You said 'one or two clear changes remain.' One or two is enough. 21 days don't rewrite a lifetime — what matters is that one or two anchors are firmly set.

[Axis D · Assetization ⭐]

You said a 'faint sense' remains. A faint sense doesn't disappear — over time, it returns in some form. The smallest seed these 21 days planted.

[Axis D · Assetization ⭐]

You said 'almost no trace remains.' Thank you for your deep honesty. And yet — the fact that you walked all 21 days and arrived here is unmistakable evidence the trace is not zero. Traces live in actions, not in awareness. The coaching call is a place to find those action-traces.
And you said you can 'explain it in one sentence.' Being able to explain it to others means it has settled within you. This sentence will be the starting line of the coaching call.
You said you can describe it 'vaguely.' Vagueness isn't negative — it's the only state that lets you go deeper. The coaching call is a place to move that vagueness into clarity.
You said it's 'still hard to explain.' Compressing a 21-day experience into one sentence is itself a tall ask. We'll craft that sentence together from scratch on the coaching call.

Your one sentence:

  "{{q12}}"

Please stay deeply with this sentence for a moment. This is the deepest place among the 12 questions. No one else could write this — it is uniquely yours.

Read this sentence again in a month, three months, a year. Each time, the same words will read differently. That's the proof of assetization. Your coach will follow this sentence's deepest grain with you on the call.

⬛ One line for your coach — [Axis D · Assetization ⭐]

Write the deepest one thing these 21 days left with you — in any form. A word, a sentence, an image. This will be the first line your coach reads. (Optional, ~500 chars)

Mission, Action, Next Three Weeks, Assetization — we have walked through these four threads together.

The shape of your 21 days is now gathered onto a single page.

This single page reaches your coach's hands before the call.

The coach keeps it close and quietly shapes the flow of the conversation in advance.

One last step — please choose a time for your 1:1 coaching call.

When you press [📞 Book 1:1 Coaching] below, you can pick a time that feels right for you. If there is a single line you would like your coach to read in advance, please leave it as well.

Thank you for walking the full 21 days.
